questions.question: Which of the following methods is suitable for solving the n-Queens problem, which involves placing N queens on an NxN chessboard such that no two queens attack each other?
questions.explanation: Backtracking is a suitable method for solving the n-Queens problem as it allows the algorithm to explore all possible configurations

questions.question: Which of the following problems can be solved efficiently using the Branch-and-Bound method?
questions.explanation: The Branch-and-Bound method is particularly effective in solving problems like the Knapsack problem, where the goal is to maximize the value of items in a knapsack with limited capacity

questions.question: Approximation algorithms are typically used in which class of NP-hard problems?
questions.explanation: Approximation algorithms are often used for solving NP-hard problems like the Knapsack problem or the Traveling salesman problem, where the optimal solution may be difficult to compute exactly

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true about the Subset-sum problem?
options: A) It is an NP-complete problem
B) It is an NP-hard problem
C) It is an NP-intermediate problem
questions.explanation: The Subset-sum problem is actually in P, meaning that the optimal solution can be computed in polynomial time

questions.question: Which of the following methods is NOT typically used to solve NP-hard problems?
questions.explanation: Greedy algorithms are generally not effective in solving NP-hard problems, as they often fail to consider all possible solutions
